summary: Backend: 25/25 pytest tests pass (REST + WebSocket) covering auth, items CRUD/filters/likes, favorites, my-listings, upload, files, requests booking flow (own-item guard, accept w/ custom_price, reject, cancel + system message), conversations, messages w/ unread counts, WS real-time delivery, reviews/public profile. Frontend: All key pages render (Home, ItemDetail w/ map + carousel + calendar, Requests tabs, Messages, Chat, Post, MyListings, Profile). Search filter, category filter, like toggle, owner card, calendar date-range selection (3 days × $3 = $9 total) and Send Request POST all functionally correct when triggered programmatically. CRITICAL BUG: 'Made with Emergent' badge (a tag, z-index 9999, 178x40 at bottom-right) overlays the sticky CTA bar so the right-side action buttons — Send Request on /item/:id and Send Message on /chat/:id — are click-blocked by the badge. Verified by element bbox: button at x=202-374,y=770-828 vs badge at x=196-374,y=788-828. JS-dispatched click bypasses the overlay and the underlying handlers work perfectly (request created and navigated to /requests, toast 'Request sent to owner!' shown).

minor.issue: No authorization check — anyone with a valid session can POST status=cancelled on any request_id. accepted/rejected enforce owner_id==user but cancel path does not check requester_id/owner_id membership.

Deleting a rented/booked item leaves orphaned requests and conversations referencing the gone item (item gets resolved as null in enrich_request). Consider blocking delete on active rentals or cascading.

ui_bugs.component: ItemDetail sticky CTA + Chat input bar
ui_bugs.issue: Made with Emergent badge (z-index 9999, ~178x40, bottom-right) overlays the right half of the sticky bottom CTA, click-blocking [data-testid='send-request'] on /item/:id and [data-testid='send-message'] on /chat/:id. Users on real mobile cannot send a booking request or send a chat message via tap.

issues: Sticky bottom CTA at z-index 40 sits behind Emergent branding badge — add ~48px bottom safe-area padding to bottom bars, or shift CTAs upward, so badge does not overlap interactive controls.

action_items: CRITICAL UI: Resolve overlap between 'Made with Emergent' badge (z=9999, bottom-right) and sticky CTAs. Suggest: add pb-[60px] to sticky CTA bars on ItemDetail and Chat input, OR shift send-request/send-message to left side, OR pin badge to a non-conflict zone.
BACKEND: Add authorization check to PUT /api/requests/{id}/status cancelled path (must be requester or owner).
BACKEND (nice-to-have): Prevent item delete when active accepted requests exist, or cascade-cancel them with system message.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_request_status: 'cancelled' branch is missing owner/requester check (security gap).
server.py auth_me / get_current_user: cookie-then-Bearer fallback works correctly. WS auth via query token or cookie OK.
Sticky CTA z-index (40) is lower than Emergent badge (9999) — add bottom safe padding rather than racing z-indexes.
All data-testid attributes are present and consistent. No MongoDB _id leakage observed.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all pages render and integrate; 2 critical CTAs blocked by overlay)

test_credentials: Session-based via cookie/Bearer. Pre-seeded for tests: session_token='qa_session_main' (user_qa_main) and session_token='qa_session_alt' (user_qa_alt) in MongoDB test_database. Seed owners user_seed_0/1/2 with 4 items.
seed_data_creation: Created two MongoDB sessions (qa_session_main/alt) + matching users (user_qa_main/alt) for two-party booking & chat tests. Test items are TEST_-prefixed and cleaned up by their own tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v. Auth seeds qa_session_main/qa_session_alt are in DB. For UI clicks on the bottom-right sticky CTAs (Send Request, Send Message), use JS-dispatched click() (page.evaluate) to bypass the Emergent badge overlay until the badge overlap is fixed. Backend handlers themselves are healthy.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Emergent branding badge is rendered as an <a> link with z-index:9999 fixed at the bottom-right of the viewport (rect 196,788 → 374,828). Sticky CTA bar uses z-index:40 and the right-side button (Send Request / Send Message) occupies bottom-right ~x=202-374, y=770-828, fully overlapping. Pointer events go to the badge, so taps never reach the button. Verified by computed style snapshot and by successfully invoking the same handler via element.click() in DevTools — request POSTed and toast shown. Mitigation: ensure no interactive content lives in the badge footprint, e.g., add pb-[60px] to sticky bars in ItemDetail.jsx and Chat.jsx, or move primary CTA to the left half, or assign the badge pointer-events:none and let z-index ordering hide it visually.
